mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer demand, which directly drives corporate revenue growth and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Over any rolling 1-year window, the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns is negative or zero; or the mean return after positive RSAFS months is not significantly greater than after negative months (t-test p < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, pushing stock prices higher over the following week.
regime_hint: early labor market recovery from recession
failure_condition: After trigger events, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns is <= 50% over the full history, or the mean 5-day return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly expansions in both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm synchronized demand-supply recovery, amplifying positive economic momentum and driving superior equity returns in the ensuing month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both post positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: When both proxies show positive growth, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI y/y below 2%), rising consumer prices indicate reflation and improved pricing power, which supports corporate profits and leads to positive long-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: During low inflation periods, the correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and 60-day forward SPX returns is negative, or the mean return after positive CPI months is not > 0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market that raises aggregate income and consumption, driving up equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward SPX return following months with PAYEMS gains is not positive, or the Spearman correlation between PAYEMS level change and forward return is <= 0 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above a threshold signals robust construction activity and consumer confidence, which lifts cyclical sectors and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Following months with HOUST above 1.5M, the 5-day SPX return is negative more often than positive, or the mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decrease in unemployment rate (UNRATE) and increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) provides strong confirmation of labor market improvement, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive monthly returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS results in negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: During the recovery phase from a deep recession, very strong GDP growth (above 5% annualized) signals rapid economic normalization that overshoots expectations, driving outsized long-horizon equity gains.
failure_condition: After quarters with GDP growth >5%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is not positive, or the mean return is lower than the unconditional mean return during non-recovery periods.
